title: Aletes hendersonii
intro: Aletes hendersonii, also known as Henderson's Aletes, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of California in the United States. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Aletes hendersonii include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Aletes hendersonii has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.

This family is part of the larger clade of angiosperms, or flowering plants. The genus Aletes itself is often included in the genus Angelica, although some sources may still recognize it as a distinct genus.
